species: New intervention strategies are required to explore the midgut proteins and their potential role in refractoriness for malaria control in Anopheles mosquitoes.
tissue: the midgut of sugar fed An. culicifacies species A (susceptible) and An. culicifacies species B (refractory).
disease_state: Midgut invasion, a major bottleneck for malaria parasites transmission...
sample_source: sugar fed An. culicifacies species A and An. culicifacies species B
The present study was carried out on the midgut of sugar fed An. culicifacies species A (susceptible) and An. culicifacies species B (refractory).
age: Mosquitoes analyzed were 2–3-day old sugar fed An. culicifacies species A and An. culicifacies species B.
strain: P. vivax refractory strain An. culicifacies species B
Establishment of refractory strain of An. culicifacies species B was previously described.
